Return: file paths, key function names, and the full content of the prompt-related files.

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.

29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.

33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`

37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`

39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`

51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
53		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
54		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`

58		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
59		// non-landing outcomes.

61	
62		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
63		Reason      string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
64		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
65		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
66		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
67		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
68		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.

177	// harnessAliases maps convenience names to canonical harness names.
178	// "local" always routes to the embedded Fizeau harness; it must never
179	// fall through to a cloud harness like claude or codex.
180	var harnessAliases = map[string]string{
181		"local": "agent",
182	}
183	
184	// resolveHarnessAlias returns the canonical harness name for an alias,
185	// or the input unchanged if it is not an alias.
186	func resolveHarnessAlias(name string) string {
187		if canonical, ok := harnessAliases[name]; ok {
188			return canonical
189		}
190		return name
191	}

193	// harnessRegistry manages known harnesses.
194	type harnessRegistry struct {
195		LookPath  LookPathFunc
196		harnesses map[string]harnessConfig
197	}
198	
199	// newHarnessRegistry creates a registry with builtin harnesses.
200	func newHarnessRegistry() *harnessRegistry {
201		r := &harnessRegistry{
202			LookPath:  DefaultLookPath,
203			harnesses: make(map[string]harnessConfig),
204		}
205		for k, v := range builtinHarnessConfigs {
206			r.harnesses[k] = v
207		}
208		return r
209	}
210	
211	// Get returns a harness by name.
212	func (r *harnessRegistry) Get(name string) (harnessConfig, bool) {
213		h, ok := r.harnesses[name]
214		return h, ok
215	}
216	
217	// Has returns true if the harness is registered.
218	func (r *harnessRegistry) Has(name string) bool {
219		_, ok := r.harnesses[name]
220		return ok
221	}

172	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
173	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
174	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
175	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.

177	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
178	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
179		FromRev         string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
180		PromptFile      string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
181		Output          io.Writer
182		WorkerID        string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
183		BeadEvents      BeadEventAppender
184		BeadCancel      BeadCancelStore // optional: enables operator-cancel mid-attempt poll
185		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
186		Service         agentlib.FizeauService
187		AgentRunner     AgentRunner
188		// RateLimitMaxWait bounds the per-bead total wait spent on rate-limit
189		// retries (ddx-c6e3db02 RateLimitRetryContract / TD-031 §8.4). Zero uses
190		// RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget (5 min). Negative disables the wrapper —
191		// rate-limit responses fall through to the standard execution_failed path.
192		RateLimitMaxWait time.Duration

195	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
196	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
197	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
198	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
199	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
200	// worktree.
201	type GitOps interface {
202		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
203		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
204		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
205		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
206		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
207		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
208		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
209		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
210		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
211		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
212		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
213		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
214		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
215		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
216		// other work that might prune it.
217		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
218		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
219		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
220		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
221	}

3	// prompt_file_read.go centralizes the bounded read for user-supplied
4	// --prompt files (FEAT-022 §8). All agent-side prompt-file readers
5	// route through readPromptFileBounded so that oversize inputs hard-fail
6	// with an actionable error rather than silently truncating or being
7	// shipped to a provider as-is.
8	
9	import (
10		"errors"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
14	)
15	
16	// promptFileCapBytes is the byte cap applied to user-supplied --prompt
17	// files. It exists as a package-level variable (rather than a literal)
18	// so tests can lower the cap to keep oversize fixtures small. Production
19	// callers must not mutate it.
20	var promptFileCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
21	
22	// readPromptFileBounded reads path as a user-supplied prompt file,
23	// hard-failing when the file exceeds the configured prompt cap.
24	// On oversize the returned error names the source path, observed size,
25	// configured cap, and points the operator at the config key that
26	// adjusts the cap (.ddx/config.yaml:evidence_caps.max_prompt_bytes).
27	func readPromptFileBounded(path string) ([]byte, error) {
28		cap := promptFileCapBytes
29		content, err := evidence.ReadFileHardFail(path, cap, path)
30		if err != nil {
31			if errors.Is(err, evidence.ErrOversize) {
32				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
33					"prompt file %q exceeds cap: %v (configurable via .ddx/config.yaml:evidence_caps.max_prompt_bytes)",
34					path, err)
35			}
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		return content, nil
39	}

- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record edges.
3. ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `.
4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.`

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
